findings.title: Trigger name set_updated_at reused across 9 tables — DROP TRIGGER order-sensitive
findings.why_it_matters: Every table reuses the trigger name `set_updated_at`. This is fine per PostgreSQL — trigger names are table-scoped, not schema-scoped. Each `DROP TRIGGER IF EXISTS set_updated_at ON public.<table>` targets the specific table. No functional issue. Advisory: future engineers unfamiliar with PostgreSQL trigger scoping may be confused by the repeated name.
evidence: Line 72-75: `DROP TRIGGER IF EXISTS set_updated_at ON public.notes` — table-scoped correctly
Matching P0 migration pattern — consistent naming is correct here
